rows.definition: internal/agent's HTTPPlanner is the single outbound chat-completions HTTP client that implements agent.Planner, speaking the OpenAI/Anthropic/Gemini wire to a live upstream model.
rows.distinction: It is the network-backed planner that calls a remote model, NOT the in-kernel InKernelPlanner that decodes a local GGUF nor the offline MockPlanner.

rows.definition: internal/agent/mock.go's MockPlanner is a deterministic, offline agent.Planner that emulates a real planner with a fixed scripted sequence, used in CI and as cmd/fak/main.go's fallback.
rows.distinction: It is scripted and offline, NOT a live network planner (HTTPPlanner) and NOT the internal/agenttest ScriptedPlanner test fixture.

rows.definition: internal/agent's StreamingPlanner is the optional interface a Planner advertises (CompleteStream/StreamingSupported) when it can emit completion tokens incrementally.
rows.distinction: It is a capability interface a planner may also implement, NOT a concrete planner like HTTPPlanner nor the gateway's Anthropic SSE bridge streamAnthropicPlannerLive.

rows.definition: swebench's CodePlanner is the domain interface for a code-edit planner over ChatMessage/ChatTool; cmd/fak's httpCodePlanner adapts an agent.HTTPPlanner to it.
rows.distinction: It is the swebench-specific code-edit planner contract, NOT the generic agent.Planner nor the fleet wrapper FleetPlanner.

rows.definition: cmd/fak's newFleetPlanner (and the swebench config FleetPlanner field) builds the swebench fleet runner's planner from a gateway address and model.
rows.distinction: It is the fleet-runner's per-worker planner built over a gateway, NOT the single CodePlanner interface it satisfies.

rows.definition: internal/agent's SessionPlanner.PlanTurn ingests new messages incrementally, then probes a bounded candidate set to produce a ctxplan resident-context Plan for the turn.
rows.distinction: It plans the resident CONTEXT WINDOW for a session, NOT the chat completion itself (HTTPPlanner) nor a memory-fit plan.

rows.definition: internal/gateway's sessionPlanners is the Server's bounded map holding one persistent agent.SessionPlanner per session trace id, minted lazily by sessionPlannerFor.
rows.distinction: It is the gateway-side per-trace CACHE of planners, NOT the agent.SessionPlanner type or its PlanTurn method.

rows.definition: internal/agent's CtxViewPlanner is the guarded seam (gated by FAK_CTXPLAN_SEAM) whose PlanTurn lowers a turn's running messages into a lossless ctxplan View; disabled, it is an inert identity.
rows.distinction: It is the agent-side on/off seam into the ctxplan engine, NOT the gateway-side hook maybePlanMessages that drives it.

rows.definition: internal/gateway's Server.maybePlanMessages is the per-request hook that, when the ctxplan seam is enabled, rewrites the forwarded message history via the per-session planner; otherwise it returns the history byte-for-byte unchanged.
rows.distinction: It is the gateway integration hook into the ctxplan seam, NOT the agent-side CtxViewPlanner type it delegates to.

rows.definition: internal/compute's MemoryPlan is the classed form of a 'will this fit?' estimate — a list of MemoryDemand rows split into device- and host-scoped totals.
rows.distinction: It is a memory-capacity plan checked against a backend, NOT a context-window plan (ctxplan) nor a routed model plan (modelroute).

rows.definition: internal/compute's FitsMemoryPlan returns a fail-open fit verdict for a classed MemoryPlan, checking device-scoped bytes against the device probe and host-scoped bytes against the host probe.
rows.distinction: It is the pure fit VERDICT over a MemoryPlan, NOT the MemoryPlan data itself nor the erroring wrapper RefuseMemoryPlanIfTooBig.

rows.definition: internal/compute's RefuseMemoryPlanIfTooBig turns a too-big MemoryPlan into an error (refusal) against a backend's device headroom, used by serve and the in-kernel planner before admitting a request.
rows.distinction: It is the refusing wrapper that ERRORS, NOT the pure verdict FitsMemoryPlan.

rows.definition: internal/ggufload's WeightSource.EstimateLoadMemoryPlan builds a classed compute.MemoryPlan for loading a GGUF model's weights grouped by dtype; the F32-resident and CPU-offload-experts variants specialise it.
rows.distinction: It is the WEIGHT-load estimator that produces a MemoryPlan, NOT compute's KV/transient estimators (EstimateKVStoreMemoryPlan).

rows.definition: internal/compute's EstimateKVStoreMemoryPlan is the classed form of the KV-cache store estimate; EstimateHALTransientMemoryPlan is the matching transient/scratch-buffer estimator.
rows.distinction: These estimate KV-cache and transient scratch demands, NOT the model weight-load demand (EstimateLoadMemoryPlan in ggufload).

rows.definition: internal/gateway's requestMemoryPlanSnapshot is a captured record of the in-kernel planner's per-request memory plan (class/dtype demand rows), accumulated for the debug and metrics surfaces.
rows.distinction: It is a gateway metrics SNAPSHOT of a request's memory plan, NOT the compute.MemoryPlan type it is derived from.

rows.definition: internal/gateway's Server.observePlannerRequestMemory samples the in-kernel planner's last per-request memory plan into the gateway's Prometheus gauges.
rows.distinction: It is the observe/sample method that publishes metrics, NOT the requestMemoryPlanSnapshot record it reads.

rows.definition: internal/gateway's Server.plannerErrorStatus maps an error returned by the upstream planner to an HTTP status, error code, and message for the chat/messages responses.
rows.distinction: It classifies a planner's ERROR into an HTTP reply, NOT the planner backend (plannerKind) nor a planner itself.

rows.definition: internal/gateway's plannerKind classifies the /v1/chat/completions backend (in-kernel / proxy / mock) as a label for the /healthz response.
rows.distinction: It names WHICH backend serves, NOT the error mapping plannerErrorStatus nor a fleet replica entry.

rows.definition: internal/gateway's PlannerReplica is one statically-declared upstream (Name + Planner) in a gateway fleet, consumed by the ReplicaRouter.
rows.distinction: It is a declared MEMBER of a fleet router, NOT the backend classifier plannerKind.

rows.definition: internal/gateway's Server.streamAnthropicPlannerLive translates a generic planner's live content stream into Anthropic /v1/messages SSE events.
rows.distinction: It is the gateway-side Anthropic SSE BRIDGE over a planner stream, NOT the planner's own StreamingPlanner capability interface.

rows.definition: internal/session's Pace.ComposePlannerBudget scales a base resident-context window down by the Pace throttle ratio, floored at base/MinPlannerBudgetDivisor.
rows.distinction: It derives the per-session planner BUDGET from a throttle, NOT the planner itself nor a cache warm-keep budget (PlanWarmBudget).

rows.definition: internal/ctxplan's PlannerComputeCum is the cumulative O(N^2) re-planning WORK through turn N in the Planned regime (computed by cumPlannerCompute) — a scaling-curve cost proxy.
rows.distinction: It is the QUADRATIC full-scan re-planning cost, NOT the index-bounded linear version IndexBoundedPlannerCompute.

rows.definition: internal/ctxplan's IndexBoundedPlannerCompute is the cumulative re-planning work through turn N when each turn scores only a bounded candidate set — Theta(c*N), flattening the quadratic cumPlannerCompute.
rows.distinction: It is the BOUNDED linear re-planning cost, NOT the quadratic PlannerComputeCum it flattens.

rows.definition: internal/ctxplan's Index.PlanCells selects spans within a token Budget from a Forecast to produce a resident-context Plan.
rows.distinction: It is the flat span-budget planner, NOT the four-area PlanLayout nor a cost metric.

rows.definition: internal/ctxplan's Index.PlanLayout plans an O(1) resident view using the flexible four-area layout (e.g. Recent/Deep areas with per-area policies).
rows.distinction: It plans by LAYOUT AREAS, NOT the flat span-budget selection of PlanCells.

rows.definition: internal/ctxplan's PrecisionPlanned ('planned') is the span precision meaning a normal candidate that may be selected exactly or elided by the optimizer.
rows.distinction: It is a per-span precision enum value, NOT PrecisionExact/PrecisionPointer and NOT a planning function.

rows.definition: internal/agent's PlannedTokens is the prompt+max-new token count the in-kernel planner's per-request memory plan is sized for, recorded on the request-memory observation.
rows.distinction: It is the token count a MEMORY plan budgets for, NOT a ctxplan span precision (PrecisionPlanned).

rows.definition: internal/ctxplan's ProbeOptions.MaxCandidates (default DefaultMaxCandidates) caps the bounded candidate set the per-turn probe scores, keeping re-planning O(1) per turn.
rows.distinction: It is the probe-set SIZE cap, NOT a benchmark candidate task nor a routing candidate.

rows.definition: internal/browseraction's CandidateTaskIDs is the list of benchmark task ids selected for an action-mediation contract, alongside CandidateSuite and CandidateTasks.
rows.distinction: These are benchmark task-SELECTION candidates, NOT a ctxplan probe candidate (MaxCandidates) nor a routing candidate.

rows.definition: internal/browseraction's ContractTaskCandidate is one task record in a contract's candidate set (built by contractTaskCandidates; candidateTaskDetail renders its count).
rows.distinction: It is a single candidate TASK record, NOT the id-list field CandidateTaskIDs.

rows.definition: internal/agent's CandidatesTokenCount is the Gemini usage-metadata field counting tokens across the model's response candidates, mapped into CompletionTokens.
rows.distinction: Its 'candidate' is a Gemini RESPONSE candidate (an output), NOT a plan/routing candidate.

rows.definition: internal/turnbench's TopologyCandidate is a scored multi-agent topology shape produced by the topology search.
rows.distinction: It is a benchmark TOPOLOGY genome, NOT a policy-search genome (SearchCandidate) nor a routing candidate.

rows.definition: internal/turnbench's SearchCandidate is one searched policy genome plus its measured fitness in the policy search.
rows.distinction: It is a POLICY-search genome, NOT the topology-search TopologyCandidate.

rows.definition: internal/webbench's pythonCandidates returns the ordered list of python interpreter names/paths to probe when locating a usable interpreter.
rows.distinction: Its 'candidates' are filesystem interpreter choices, NOT plan/routing candidates.

rows.definition: internal/cachemeta's PlanTemplate is an advisory, reusable plan-template cache artifact keyed by intent (media type MediaPlanTemplate, carrying a PlanSchemaDigest), reused across turns like a prepared statement.
rows.distinction: It is a reusable AGENT-plan cache template (a HIT must re-enter plancfi), NOT a memory-fit plan (MemoryPlan) nor a ctxplan view.

rows.definition: internal/plancfi is control-flow integrity for an agent's PLAN — a stateful per-trace adjudicator that refuses (or escalates) a tool call that deviates from the operator-approved plan.
rows.distinction: It ENFORCES conformance of tool calls to an approved plan (a security gate), NOT a planner that produces a plan, and NOT a reusable plan cache (PlanTemplate).

rows.definition: BuildPlan is the verb-named function (in internal/dogfoodissues and internal/stopfailure) that diffs inputs into a concrete list of actions — e.g. create-vs-update GitHub issues, or stale-session resets.
rows.distinction: It builds an ACTION list from a diff, NOT a reusable plan cache (PlanTemplate) nor a context-window plan (PlanCells).

rows.definition: internal/nightrun's PlanReport is the stable --json shape emitted by `fak nightrun plan` — the ranked overnight collection-task plan.
rows.distinction: It is the JSON REPORT of the nightrun plan, NOT the BuildPlan action builder.

rows.definition: internal/dropin's PlanFor composes the drop-in resolution plan for a named CLI agent (claude/codex/...) against a gateway, autodetecting the provider.
rows.distinction: It plans how to WIRE a drop-in CLI to fak, NOT an issue/action plan (BuildPlan) nor a context plan.

rows.definition: internal/engine's PlanPlacementForDevice plans a cachemeta placement decision (keep / demote / evict) for a request against the device that actually exists and its resident bytes.
rows.distinction: It plans a per-device PLACEMENT decision, NOT a memory-fit plan (MemoryPlan) nor fleet-reuse economics (PlanFleetReuse).

rows.definition: internal/cachemeta's PlanFleetReuse computes the three-way fleet economics for one hot prefix shared across the fleet (a deterministic, payload-free calculation).
rows.distinction: It plans FLEET-wide reuse of a shared prefix, NOT a single device's placement (PlanPlacementForDevice).

rows.definition: internal/vcachegov's PlanWarmBudget computes the warm-keep token budget for a cache tier from its headroom, RPM, and TTL (the keep-warm crossover).
rows.distinction: It is a cache WARM-KEEP budget, NOT the planner context-window budget ComposePlannerBudget.

rows.definition: internal/model's PartitionPlan (built by NewPartitionPlan) is a pipeline-partition plan that cuts a model's layers into pipeline stages.
rows.distinction: It is the PIPELINE-parallel inter-layer partition, NOT the tensor-parallel intra-layer sharding TPPlan.

rows.definition: internal/model's NewTPPlan builds a tensor-parallel plan: the row/column tiling of a layer's matrices across TP ranks.
rows.distinction: It is TENSOR-parallel intra-layer sharding, NOT the pipeline-parallel layer partition PartitionPlan.

rows.definition: internal/modelroute's ResolvedPlan binds a routed Plan to concrete targets (the optional Scout plus the resolved roster), produced by Roster.ResolvePlan.
rows.distinction: It is a ROUTING plan bound to concrete model targets, NOT a memory plan or a context plan.

rows.definition: internal/vcachechain's ReplayPlan is the topological replay schedule (send-one-then-fan) for warming a set of cache targets, produced by PrefixDAG.TopologicalReplay.
rows.distinction: It is a cache-warm REPLAY schedule, NOT a routing plan (ResolvedPlan) nor the test-only ReplayPlanner.

rows.definition: internal/promptmmu's ToolPlan is the spine's pure, caller-supplied verdict (e.g. a Drop set) over a request's tools, driving inbound tool compaction.
rows.distinction: It is a tool-COMPACTION drop plan, NOT a reusable agent plan template (PlanTemplate).

rows.definition: internal/dogfoodissues.BuildPlanWithOptions builds dogfood issue-sync rows while applying the shared issue-candidate contract and dedupe options.
rows.distinction: It builds an issue-sync PLAN from action items, NOT a dispatch launch plan and NOT the generic BuildPlan action planner.

rows.definition: internal/appversion.DefaultBinaryDoctorCandidates returns the ordered binary-name candidates the version doctor should inspect for a fak executable.
rows.distinction: It is a binary-discovery CANDIDATE list, NOT dispatch issue candidates and NOT a launch plan.

rows.definition: internal/compute.ExpertParallelPerRankPlan builds the per-rank MemoryPlan for expert-parallel serving by splitting expert weights across ranks and adding replicated and per-rank extras.
rows.distinction: It is a MEMORY capacity plan for expert-parallel serving, NOT dispatch wave execution planning and NOT a context plan.

rows.definition: internal/dispatchtick.OrderLaneCandidates sorts candidate issue numbers for a lane using priority tier, optional recency preference, and issue number tie-breaks.
rows.distinction: It orders issue CANDIDATES within one lane, NOT worker accounts and NOT the launch wave schedule.

rows.definition: internal/issuecontract.ReviewCandidate grades one issue candidate against dispatchability, scope, and live-sync safeguards before it can be posted or synced.
rows.distinction: It is an issue-candidate REVIEW gate, NOT a dispatch execution plan and NOT a human code review.

rows.definition: The dispatch price and live telemetry CandidateCount field is the number of candidates admitted to the price calculation after routing, held-lane, cooldown, and scope filters.
rows.distinction: It counts PRICED dispatch candidates, NOT safe concurrency, not issue totals, and not binary doctor candidates.

rows.definition: cmd/fak dispatch pricing's LaunchPlan is the wave-grouped list of dispatch launch targets produced from collision-priced candidates.
rows.distinction: It is a DRY launch target schedule, NOT the live execution plan that binds account seats and dispatch tick commands.

rows.definition: cmd/fak dispatch wave's ExecutionPlan binds launch targets to account seats, worker backend, work kind, and the exact dispatch tick command that would run.
rows.distinction: It is the EXECUTABLE worker plan, NOT the dry LaunchPlan emitted by dispatch price.

rows.definition: The dispatch PlanID is the stable short sha256 digest label for a JSON-serialized launch or execution plan.
rows.distinction: It is a DIGEST label for a plan payload, NOT the plan body and NOT an issue number.

rows.definition: cmd/fak dispatch wave's execution_plan_id is the stable digest label for the account-bound execution plan checked by the prelaunch gate.
rows.distinction: It identifies the EXECUTION plan, NOT the dry dispatch price PlanID.

rows.definition: cmd/fak.dispatchWaveExecutionPlans converts priced dispatch wave targets and allocated account lanes into account-bound ExecutionPlan rows.
rows.distinction: It is the BUILDER for execution-plan rows, NOT the plan ID and NOT the launch-plan materializer.

rows.definition: cmd/fak.dispatchLaunchPlanFromWaves is the shared helper that materializes dispatchLaunchWave rows from priced wave IDs and a target lookup function.
rows.distinction: It materializes LAUNCH plan rows, NOT execution-plan rows and NOT the stable plan digest.

rows.definition: cmd/fak.dispatchStablePlanID hashes a JSON-serialized dispatch plan payload into the stable plan-* identifier used by price and wave reports.
rows.distinction: It computes the DIGEST, NOT the launch plan and NOT the execution plan.

rows.definition: In fak's refusal/routing vocabulary (cmd/fak/commit.go, internal/gateway/routing.go) to 'replan' is the recovery action when a commit or route is BLOCKED — discard the attempt and compute a fresh plan rather than retrying it verbatim.
rows.distinction: It is a recovery DISPOSITION an agent takes, NOT a function that builds a plan (BuildPlan).
